output.summary: Move the video modal inside each <section> element (remove the static singleton guard), restructure the close button to use normal document flow with right-alignment above the video (no absolute positioning), and add overflow-y scrolling to the modal content area for short viewports. Rework JS to scope modal operations per-section instead of using a single global modal.

fileChanges.description: Move modal inside <section>, remove static guard, restructure close button layout, add scrollability
fileChanges.details: 1. REMOVE the static $testimonial_modal_rendered guard (lines 112-116) and the corresponding endif (line 139). 2. MOVE the modal <div> to just before the closing </section> tag (line 110), so it lives INSIDE the section. Each block instance will now render its own modal. 3. RESTRUCTURE the modal inner wrapper: change from `relative w-full max-w-4xl mx-4` to `w-full max-w-4xl mx-4` (remove `relative`). Add `overflow-y-auto max-h-full py-8` to this wrapper so the entire inner content scrolls when the viewport is too short. 4. RESTRUCTURE the close button: Remove `absolute -top-12 right-0 md:-top-14 z-10` classes. Instead, wrap the close button in a `flex justify-end mb-3` container div so it sits in normal flow, right-aligned above the video area. The button keeps its `modal-close-btn` class and the SVG icon. 5. The outer modal div keeps `fixed inset-0 z-[9999] flex items-center justify-center` but the inner wrapper changes to allow scrolling.

fileChanges.description: Rework JS to scope modal per-section instead of using a single global modal
fileChanges.details: 1. REMOVE the single global modal query: `document.querySelector('.testimonial-video-modal')` and the single `videoContainer`/`closeBtn` references (lines 44-46). 2. CHANGE video button click handler: Instead of delegating to a single global modal, each `.testimonial-video-btn` click should find the modal within the same `<section>` ancestor using `btn.closest('section.testimonials-carousel').querySelector('.testimonial-video-modal')`. 3. REFACTOR openModal/closeModal: These functions should accept the modal element as a parameter (or use a `currentModal` variable that tracks which modal is open). The `openModal(modal, videoSource, videoUrl, testimonialName)` function finds `.testimonial-video-container` and `.testimonial-video-close` within the passed modal. 4. CHANGE closeModal: Should reference the currently-open modal (tracked via a module-level `let currentModal = null` variable). 5. CHANGE Escape key handler: Check `currentModal` instead of the single `modal` reference; call closeModal on whichever modal is currently open. 6. CHANGE focus trap: Scope to `currentModal` instead of the single `modal`. 7. CHANGE close button binding: Instead of binding once to a single closeBtn, bind close buttons inside each section or use event delegation. Best approach: use event delegation on `document` for `.testimonial-video-close` clicks. 8. CHANGE click-outside-to-close: Instead of binding to a single modal, use event delegation or bind to each modal. Best approach: bind in openModal and unbind in closeModal, or use a document-level click handler that checks `currentModal`. 9. UPDATE scale transition selectors: The current code uses `modal.querySelector('.relative')` to toggle scale-95/scale-100. Since we removed `relative` from the inner wrapper, update the selector to use a specific class like `.testimonial-modal-content` added to the inner wrapper div.

fileChanges.description: No changes needed to .modal-close-btn styles -- the existing styles work correctly for normal-flow positioning. The button already has inline-flex, sizing, border, and colors defined.
fileChanges.details: The .modal-close-btn class (lines 93-105) uses inline-flex, fixed sizing (w-10 h-10 / md:w-12 md:h-12), border, rounded-full, and color styles. These all work correctly whether the button is absolutely positioned or in normal flow. No CSS changes required.

designDecisions.decision: Move modal inside <section> so each block instance owns its own modal
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested: 'Move the video modal inside the <section> element -- each block instance should have its own modal (remove the static guard)'

designDecisions.decision: Close button uses flex justify-end in a wrapper div above the video, not absolute positioning
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested: 'Right-align the close button above the video area. It should NOT be absolutely positioned overlapping the video -- it should be in normal flow, aligned to the right above the video container.'

designDecisions.decision: Use a flex justify-end wrapper div with mb-3 spacing below close button row
designDecisions.evidence: Standard pattern for right-aligned elements in normal flow. The mb-3 (12px) provides visual separation between the close button and the video area without being too large.

designDecisions.decision: Modal inner wrapper gets overflow-y-auto and max-h-full with py-8 for scroll padding
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Add scrollability to the modal when the viewport is too short to fit the video -- the modal content area needs overflow-y: auto / scrollbars so users can scroll to see the full video.'

designDecisions.decision: JS uses a module-level currentModal variable to track which modal is open, and scopes all operations to that modal
designDecisions.evidence: With per-section modals, the JS can no longer reference a single global modal. A currentModal variable allows Escape key, click-outside, and focus-trap handlers to operate on whichever modal is currently open.

designDecisions.decision: Video button finds its modal via btn.closest('section').querySelector('.testimonial-video-modal') rather than document-level query
designDecisions.evidence: Each section now has its own modal. The button must find the modal within its own section ancestor to open the correct one.

designDecisions.decision: Add .testimonial-modal-content class to the inner wrapper div for JS targeting, replacing the fragile .relative selector
designDecisions.evidence: Current JS uses modal.querySelector('.relative') to toggle scale transitions. Since we're removing the `relative` class from the inner wrapper, we need a stable class name for JS to target.

phpChanges.currentStructure: Modal is rendered AFTER </section> with a static guard so only one modal exists on the page. Close button is absolutely positioned with -top-12 above the video area.
phpChanges.newStructure: Modal is rendered INSIDE <section>, before </section>. No static guard -- each block instance gets its own modal. Close button is in a flex justify-end wrapper div in normal document flow above the video. Inner wrapper has overflow-y-auto for scrollability.
modalHtmlChanges: Remove static $testimonial_modal_rendered guard and its if/endif
Move modal div to just before </section> closing tag

Inner wrapper: change from 'relative w-full max-w-4xl mx-4 scale-95 transition-transform duration-300' to 'testimonial-modal-content w-full max-w-4xl mx-4 overflow-y-auto max-h-full py-8 scale-95 transition-transform duration-300'

Close button: Remove 'absolute -top-12 right-0 md:-top-14 z-10', wrap in '
...
'

jsChanges.currentApproach: Single global modal queried via document.querySelector('.testimonial-video-modal'). openModal/closeModal reference this single element. Event listeners bound once to this single modal.
jsChanges.newApproach: Module-level `let currentModal = null` tracks the active modal. Each video button finds its section's modal. openModal sets currentModal, closeModal clears it. Escape/click-outside/focus-trap all reference currentModal.
specificChanges: Replace single modal/videoContainer/closeBtn declarations with `let currentModal = null; let lastFocusedElement = null;`
openModal(modal, videoSource, videoUrl, testimonialName): set currentModal = modal, find videoContainer and closeBtn within modal
closeModal(): operate on currentModal, then set currentModal = null
Video button click: find modal via btn.closest('section').querySelector('.testimonial-video-modal'), call openModal with it
Close button: use event delegation -- document.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target.closest('.testimonial-video-close')) closeModal(); })
Click outside: document-level handler checks if click target is the currentModal overlay itself
Escape key: check currentModal !== null instead of checking single modal classes
Scale transition: use '.testimonial-modal-content' selector instead of '.relative'

qaChecklist: Video modal opens when clicking 'Watch the Video' button on any testimonial card
Modal opens within the correct section (inspect DOM to verify modal is inside <section>)
Close button appears right-aligned above the video, in normal document flow (not overlapping video)
Close button click closes the modal
Clicking outside the video area (on the dark overlay) closes the modal
Body scroll is locked when modal is open
Body scroll is restored when modal is closed
Focus moves to close button when modal opens
Focus returns to the video button when modal closes
Focus trap works (Tab cycles within modal)
Modal is scrollable when viewport height is too short for the video content
Multiple TestimonialsCarousel blocks on the same page each have their own modal and work independently
YouTube embed videos load and play correctly in modal
File-based videos load and play correctly in modal
Video content is cleared after modal close transition completes
Scale transition (scale-95 -> scale-100) still works on open/close
